title: Fill Thou my life, O Lord my God
firstLine: Fill Thou my life, O Lord my God,
publicDomainBasis: Horatius Bonar (1808–1889), Thomas Haweis (1734–1820). All public domain.

sections.label: Verse 1
sections.text:
Fill Thou my life, O Lord my God,
In every part with praise,
That my whole being may proclaim
Thy being and Thy ways.

sections.label: Verse 2
sections.text:
Not for the lip of praise alone,
Nor e’en the praising heart,
I ask, but for a life made up
Of praise in every part:

sections.label: Verse 3
sections.text:
Praise in the common things of life,
Its goings out and in;
Praise in each duty and each deed,
However small and mean.

sections.label: Verse 4
sections.text:
Fill every part of me with praise;
Let all my being speak
Of Thee and of Thy love, O Lord,
Poor though I be and weak.

sections.label: Verse 5
sections.text:
So shall no part of day or night
From sacredness be free,
But all my life, in every step,
Be fellowship with Thee.
